strict_gate.breakdown: of C4's 10 blocking: 4 (constant/voltage/probe/cantilever) removed by repair; 6 (poly(S-r-DIB)/HERO/E/F/ISPD/Vs) declared as known-FP and now suppressed. Net blocking 10 -> 0.

regression_verdict: PASS (machine, clean): the 3 label moves removed exactly the 4 real strict-blocking defects with zero new candidates, page geometry unchanged, all other detectors identical, boundary coverage unchanged at 2. The 6 confirmed false positives were declared via the existing fixture-scoped known-FP mechanism (glyph-scoped, tight bounds). Strict compile is GREEN for the first time -- a MACHINE-VALID layer only, not visual/human/release/publication acceptance.
false_positive_suppression_safety: each FP pattern is fixture-scoped AND glyph-scoped with a dark_max just above the measured signal, so it cannot suppress the 4 real repaired defects (different glyphs, dark 0.11-0.21) nor a future stronger collision on the same glyph. Verified: with the patterns present, raw (un-suppressed) detection still reports all candidates; only --ignore-known-fp hides the 6.
